svg.title: WhatsApp app open sequence after notification
svg.desc: Shows the parallel paths that execute when the app foregrounds: TCP reconnect, queue flush, ACK cascade, and UI resolution from 'maybe' to definite count

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the OS do when you tap a WhatsApp notification?')
sendPrompt('How does WhatsApp re-establish its TCP connection when the app opens?')
sendPrompt('What does WhatsApp send to the server when it registers presence?')
sendPrompt('How does WhatsApp flush the message queue when you reconnect?')
g.text: all held messages pushed in order

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does WhatsApp show while it is reconnecting after a notification?')
sendPrompt('How does WhatsApp update the unread count in real time?')
g.text: unread count resolves to exact number

g.onclick: sendPrompt('When does WhatsApp send a read receipt automatically?')
g.text: fired if chat is already on screen

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is a stanza ACK in WhatsApp and when is it sent?')
sendPrompt('How does the sender get notified when their message is delivered?')
sendPrompt('How do grey ticks turn blue on the sender side?')
sendPrompt('Why does WhatsApp say you may have new messages instead of a count?')
g.text: Exact count shown, ticks accurate, queue empty

svg.text: Typical wall-clock time: 200–800ms on a good connection
TCP + Noise handshake dominates. Queue flush is fast — it was already waiting.
